notes:
vMLX 1.6.13

Streaming-failure integrity and packaged-runtime isolation checkpoint.

Highlights:
- Preserves progressive partial output, authoritative terminal usage, and immediate same-chat recovery when Chat Completions or Responses fails after streaming begins.
- Emits native terminal errors for Anthropic Messages and all Ollama streaming routes without synthesizing false success after failure.
- Isolates bundled Python imports so an unrelated launch-cwd mlx directory cannot shadow the packaged runtime.
- Retains architecture-aware prefix, paged, block-disk L2, and q4 TurboQuant stored-KV behavior without conflating affine JANG, JANGTQ/MXTQ, and base MLX MXFP formats.

Verification:
- Runtime source checkpoint 5fae65d3805b1aa5cd44c9634eb754f2851b4779; tag v1.6.13.
- Full Python suite: 6185 passed, 99 skipped, 92 deselected. Full panel suite: 2336 passed, 3 skipped; TypeScript typecheck passed.
- Both DMGs passed bundled-runtime verification, Developer ID signing, fresh Apple notarization, stapling, Gatekeeper verification, and exact installed-app smoke on erics-m5-max.local.
- Sequoia completed a clean three-turn Electron reasoning/tool/recall loop, raw Responses and Chat streaming, paged+mixed-SWA+tq-native RAM reuse, and a 3359-token process-restart disk+tq-native restore. Tahoe independently completed Electron reasoning/content and raw Chat streaming with terminal usage.

Known follow-up:
- This is a public usable checkpoint, not closure of every retained family/stress row. Broader signed-app model repetition, long stochastic/media soaks, remaining parser-family tool rows, strict marker-only model reliability, and documented family-specific PARTIAL rows remain deferred.
